title: The peak of Mount Everest is 8,848.86 meters, or ... - Instagram
content: ... 8848.86 meters, or 29031.7 feet, above sea level, according to the most recent official measurement jointly announced by China and Nepal in 2020

title: New official height of Mount Everest: 8848.86 meters
content: Today the governments of Nepal and China have jointly announced: Mount Everest is currently 8,848.86 meters high – and thus about a meter higher

title: [PDF] How High Is Mount Everest
content: Notably, in 2020, Nepal and China jointly announced a new official height. Page 9. 9 of 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet), slightly higher than previous estimates

title: Mount Everest: World's Highest Peak at 8848.46 meters
content: # Mount Everest at 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet). Mount Everest, the tallest mountain in the world, is the crown jewel of the Himalayas. Located at a height of 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet), it is one of the primary locations for trekkers and mountaineers. To most, mention of the word "Everest" brings visions of white mountains, treacherous climbs, mystic monasteries, and the indomitable ambitions of climbers who wish to walk on the "roof of the world.". From a geographical point of view, it is situated on the border between Tibet (China) and Nepal, luring thousands of mountaineers to its foot annually. Culturally, it is of profound religious significance to Sherpa and Tibetan people, who regard the mountain as sacred ground, referring to it as Sagarmatha (Nepali for "Goddess of the Sky") and Chomolungma (Tibetan for "Goddess Mother of the World"). ## **What is the Height of Mount Everest in Meters?**. The official height of Mount Everest is 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 feet), making it the highest point on Earth.

title: New Height for Everest | The Blog on alanarnette.com
content: After a few years of work, and suspense, China and Nepal jointly announced that they have an updated measurement for Mt. Everest, and both countries agree on it. The Chinese used state of the art technology, while The Trigonometric Survey used basic math and raw human skills, and their measurement was a scant 9.06 meters different – amazing! * 1955 – Survey of India Service: 29,029-feet/8,848-meters (snow cap). * 1975 – Chinese measurement of 29,029.30-feet/8848.13-meters (snow cap). * 1999 – American Everest Expedition (Bradford Washburn): 29,035-feet/8850-meters (rock head; snow/ice elevation 3-feet/1-m higher). * 2010 – Nepal and China agreed on 29,029-feet/8,848 (snow cap with rock head at 29015.9-feet/8,844-meters). * 2020 – China survey team is completed taking measurements: 8848.86 -meter/ 29,031.69291-feet.
